すぐに共有を停止し、必要であればファイルを再アップロードして新しいIDとパスワー: Stop sharing immediately, and if necessary upload the file again and issue a new ID and password.
また、スマートフォンではダウンロードしたファイルが「ファイル」アプリや「ダウンロ: On smartphones, downloaded files may remain in the Files app or Downloads folder. Even on a personal device, if the screen may be shared with family or coworkers, it is safer to include checking the save location and deleting the file in the receiving steps.
また、共有先との信頼関係も忘れてはなりません。やむを得ず外部の個人と共有する場合: Don't overlook the element of trust in your sharing relationships. When you have no choice but to share with someone outside your organization, promptly deleting any information that is no longer needed and revoking access permissions minimizes the risk of a data leak.
オンラインでファイルを共有する際は、まず共有先を明確にし、目的外の人に届かないよ: When sharing files online, the first priority is to be clear about who your intended audience is — and to make sure the file doesn't reach anyone else. Sharing links tend to travel further than expected, so verify the recipient's address before sending and set viewing permissions to the minimum necessary, whether sharing internally or externally.
オンラインファイル共有の安全対策を詳しく解説。共有先の明確化、期間管理、受信環境: A detailed guide to staying secure when sharing files online. This article covers best practices for safe data exchange using FS!QR: defining your recipients, managing sharing periods, awareness of the recipient's environment, and permission settings.
ダウンロード後にどこへ保存するか、いつ削除するか、再配布してよいかを事前に決めて: Decide in advance where the file will be saved after download, when it will be deleted, and whether redistribution is allowed.
ファイル共有を安全に行うためのベストプラクティスとセキュリティのポイントを解説し: Best practices and security essentials for safe file sharing.
リンクを開く前に、送信者本人へ別の連絡手段で確認します。業務では管理者や情報シス: Before opening the link, confirm with the sender through another contact method. For work, also report it to an administrator or IT contact.
一時的な受け渡しでは、必要以上に長い保存期間を選ばない: For temporary handoff, do not choose a storage period longer than necessary.
不審な共有だと感じたら、IDやパスワードを変更するか、アップロードしたファイルを: If something about a share feels off, change your ID or password, or delete the uploaded file. Building up these basic habits is what makes genuinely secure information sharing a part of everyday routine.
不審な共有に気付いたときの対応: What to do when a share seems suspicious
共有が終わったらIDとパスワードを知っている人が不用意に再配布しないよう周知し、: Once sharing is complete, remind anyone who knows the ID and password not to pass them along carelessly, and make it standard practice to delete the file when it's no longer needed.
共有期間の管理も重要です。FS!QRではアップロードしたファイルが一定期間後に自: Managing how long files remain available is just as important. FS!QR automatically deletes uploaded files after a set period, but you should still share the ID and password only with those who need them, and encourage deletion as soon as they've finished using the file.
受け取りが完了したら、共有リンクを再利用しない: Once receipt is complete, do not reuse the shared link.
受け取り側の環境にも目を向けましょう。公共のWi-Fiや共有端末では通信内容が盗: Also consider the environment on the receiving end. Public Wi-Fi and shared devices can expose communication content to eavesdroppers, so choose a service that uses HTTPS encryption, and encourage recipients to clear their browser history and download folder promptly after accessing the file.
受け取り後の扱いが不明確: The handling after receipt is unclear
古いリンクがチャット履歴やメールに残る前提で運用する: Operate on the assumption that old links remain in chat history or email.
同じファイルを複数の相手に送る場合でも、用途ごとに共有を分ける: Even when sending the same file to several recipients, separate shares by purpose.

安全な共有のポイント | FS!QRファイル共有セキュリティガイド: Safe Sharing Best Practices | FS!QR File Sharing Security Guide
安全な共有は、強いパスワードだけでなく「必要な人に、必要な期間だけ届ける」設計で: Safe sharing depends not only on strong passwords, but also on a design that delivers files only to the right people for the necessary period.
特に、顧客資料、見積書、本人確認書類、学校や地域活動の名簿などは、相手を一人間違: Customer materials, estimates, identity documents, and school or community rosters can cause serious problems if sent to even one wrong person. Simply checking the file name, contents, recipient, and whether a password is set before sending can prevent many mistakes.
知らない相手からリンクが届いた: A link arrived from someone unknown
社内外で複数の共有方法が混在している場合は、サービスごとに操作手順が異なるため誤: When multiple sharing methods are in use across your organization — each with its own procedures — the risk of sending something to the wrong place rises. Establishing clear rules based on data sensitivity, and building shared understanding through documented procedures and training, goes a long way toward preventing confusion and accidents.
組織で共有ルールを揃える: Align sharing rules across the organization
安全な共有は、サービスの機能を有効にするだけでは完成しません。FS!QRの「必要: Safe sharing is not achieved simply by enabling a service feature. Combine FS!QR's design—delivering a file only to the people who need it, for only as long as needed—with checks before sending and cleanup after receipt. Before uploading, write down in one sentence who will use the file, for what purpose, and until when; this helps prevent unnecessary documents being mixed in or forwarded to unintended recipients.
FS!QRのアップロード画面でファイルを選んだら、送信ボタンを押す前に中身を開き: After selecting files on FS!QR's upload screen, open them and check for personal data or outdated versions before pressing Send. Match the displayed share ID, password, and retention period with the recipient's name, then share the URL or QR code only after checking. Send the ID and password through separate channels, and display the QR code only when you can verify who is receiving it, such as in person; these habits reduce hurried misdelivery.
送信者は、相手から「ダウンロードできた」と連絡を受けるまで、元のファイルを手元に: Keep the original file until the recipient confirms that the download worked. Once it has been received, confirm where it was saved and when it will be deleted, then remove the share from FS!QR as well. Do not rely only on automatic deletion: using manual deletion to remove it early is a practical way to limit forwarding and reuse of the link.
FS!QRを使わない方がよい情報: Information that should not be shared with FS!QR
法令や契約で指定された保管場所、監査ログ、厳格な本人確認が必要な情報は、勤務先や: For information that must stay in a legally or contractually specified repository, retain audit logs, or undergo strict identity verification, use the dedicated system approved by your organization or school. FS!QR is designed for temporary handoff; it is not a long-term archive or the only backup for confidential data. If you are unsure, confirm the retention period and sharing method with the data owner before using it.
ファイル共有の送信前・受信後チェックリスト: A Pre-Send and Post-Receipt Checklist for File Sharing
ファイル共有で起きやすい誤送信や期限切れを、送信前・共有中・受信後の三段階で確認: This practical checklist covers common misdirected sends and expired shares in three stages: before sending, while sharing, and after receipt. It also covers points that FS!QR's features alone cannot solve.
This practical checklist covers common misdirected sends and expired shares in three stages: before sending, while sharing, and after receipt.
ファイル共有 チェックリスト, 誤送信対策, 共有リンク, パスワード, 受信確: file-sharing checklist, misdirected-send prevention, sharing link, password, receipt confirmation, FS QR
安全なファイル共有は、暗号化されたサービスを選ぶだけでは完成しません。実際の事故: Safe file sharing is not achieved simply by choosing an encrypted service. Real incidents happen in gaps such as selecting an old version, choosing the wrong recipient, failing to check the sharing expiry, or not knowing where the received file was saved. The order of checks below works with FS!QR and with other services.
送信前：ファイルと宛先を一行で言える状態にする: Before Sending: State the File and Recipient in One Line
アップロード前に「A社の担当者へ、6月版の見積書を、金曜日までに確認してもらう」: Before uploading, write the recipient, content, and deadline in one line, such as: “Ask the contact at Company A to review the June estimate by Friday.” Then open the file and check for personal information, internal notes, old versions, or unnecessary attachments. Do not judge by the filename alone; inspect the contents.
宛先の名前と連絡先が、直近の依頼内容と一致しているか。: Do the recipient's name and contact details match the latest request?
ファイルの版、日付、ページ数が、送る目的に合っているか。: Do the file version, date, and page count fit the purpose of sending it?
共有期限が、相手の作業時間に対して長すぎたり短すぎたりしないか。: Is the sharing period too long or too short for the recipient's work?
契約や社内規程で指定された保管場所を使うべき情報ではないか。: Is this information required by a contract or internal rule to use a specified storage location?
共有中：URLとパスワードを同じ場所に置かない: While Sharing: Keep the URL and Password in Separate Places
FS!QRでは共有ページのURLとパスワードが発行されます。URLをメールで送り: FS!QR issues a URL and password for the sharing page. Consider sending the URL by email and the password through another channel, so that one message alone does not reveal both. This assumes you use contact details that confirm the recipient's identity. Splitting the channels is not the goal itself; it is a way to limit damage if a message is sent to the wrong person.
会議室のスクリーンにQRコードを表示する場合は、参加者以外が撮影できない位置かを: When displaying a QR code on a meeting-room screen, check that people outside the meeting cannot photograph it. For screen sharing in an online meeting, also consider recordings and who can attend. A URL posted on a public page or social media may already have been saved even if you delete it later.
受信前：相手が迷わない案内を添える: Before Receipt: Add Guidance That Leaves No Doubt
「リンクです」だけでなく、何のファイルか、どこを確認してほしいか、いつまでに返信: Instead of writing only “Here is the link,” say what the file is, what to check, and when a reply is needed. If the recipient was not expecting the share, have them confirm with the sender through another channel before opening it. Since phishing can impersonate file-sharing services, check that the domain matches the request.
受信後：保存先と終了条件を確認する: After Receipt: Check the Save Location and End Conditions
受信者がダウンロードできたことは、送信者側の「送信済み」表示だけでは分かりません: The sender's “sent” status alone does not show that the recipient successfully downloaded the file. For materials with a deadline, ask the recipient for a brief receipt confirmation. Check where the file was actually saved, such as the device's Downloads folder or photo app. When sharing is over, manually delete the share from the service if needed and decide how to handle copies and caches on devices.
FS!QRを使わない方がよいケース: Cases Where FS!QR Is Not the Right Choice
監査ログ、細かな閲覧権限、長期保管、厳格な本人確認、社内承認ワークフローが必要な: When you need audit logs, granular viewing permissions, long-term retention, strict identity verification, or an internal approval workflow, prioritize a dedicated system approved by your organization. FS!QR is a tool for making short-term handoffs easy. Do not assume the service specification meets your organization's requirements; check with the information owner before using it.
